mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer demand expansion, which lifts equity valuations through higher expected cash flows and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding manufacturing capacity and operational efficiency, which lowers input costs and improves profit margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with S&P 500 returns during the specified regime, or the predictive R-squared drops below 0.01 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust economic expansion, which lifts corporate earnings forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent week.
failure_condition: The threshold signal generates negative forward returns in over 60% of occurrences, or the information ratio falls below 0.5 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects sustained economic momentum, which supports equity valuations through stable earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The rolling 12-month correlation between payroll growth and S&P 500 returns remains below 0.10, or forward returns show no statistical difference from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises faster than the federal funds rate, real interest rates turn negative, compressing the present value of future cash flows and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Real rates turn negative but equity markets rally, or the interaction term loses statistical significance after controlling for volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential investment and consumer confidence, which drags down corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Housing starts fall sharply while equity markets remain flat or rise, or the signal's hit rate drops below 40% in out-of-sample testing.
